titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale Student Scrapbook Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig and staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday February 2, 2019 at 08:14

did.unittitle: Yale student scrapbook collection
did.abstract: The materials consist of scrapbooks created by and for Yale students documenting student life at Yale.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 41 (YRG 41), Material documenting Yale student life at Yale.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale Student Scrapbook Collection (RU 138). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged by accession.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of scrapbooks created by and for Yale students documenting student life at Yale.

Scrapbook of Robert Maxwell Ingham documenting the combined Yale-Harvard Track Team
Scrapbook
Scrapbook documenting James Otis Rodgers, Class of 1898

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: James Otis Rodgers, from Toledo, Ohio, attended Yale from 1894-1898. He was a member of both the crew and football teams, and coached both teams following graduation.
The scrapbook was compiled by his daughter, Carolyn Hale Rodgers, a niece of Edith Hale Harkness.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Mr. and Mrs. Willard Walker Brown, Jr., 2015. Mr. Brown received the scrapbook from his grandmother, Louise Harkness Ingalls, daughter of Edith Hale Harkness.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook
Scrapbook documenting Maurice "Red" Robert Brann, PhB 1915
Scrapbook
Scrapbooks of William Bacon Bailey, Class of 1894

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three scrapbooks documenting reunion activity and related events of the Yale College Class of 1894, of which Bailey was a member, as well as Bailey's professional life as an economist, professor, census worker, statistician, and employee at Travelers Insurance Companies.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook
Scrapbook
Scrapbook
Material removed from scrapbooks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes professional correspondence, newspaper clippings, material related to the Bailey family, drawings, and Yale ephemera.
